title: Discriminant
aria_label: D equals b squared minus 4 times a times c
notes: The discriminant is the expression inside the square root of the quadratic formula.
If D > 0: two distinct real solutions (the parabola crosses the x-axis twice).
If D = 0: exactly one real solution, a repeated root (the parabola touches the x-axis once).
If D < 0: no real solutions (the parabola does not cross the x-axis).
You can classify the number of solutions before solving — saving time on exams.

examples.description: How many real solutions does 2x² − 4x + 2 = 0 have?
How many real solutions does x² + x + 1 = 0 have?

applications: Quickly determining whether a quadratic is solvable by real methods before attempting to solve.
Predicting whether a projectile reaches a given height.
Analyzing whether two curves intersect in algebra and calculus.

es.title: Discriminante
notes: El discriminante es la expresión dentro de la raíz cuadrada de la fórmula cuadrática.
Si D > 0: dos soluciones reales distintas (la parábola cruza el eje x dos veces).
Si D = 0: exactamente una solución real, una raíz repetida (la parábola toca el eje x una vez).
Si D < 0: no hay soluciones reales (la parábola no cruza el eje x).
Puedes clasificar el número de soluciones antes de resolver — ahorrando tiempo en exámenes.

ht.title: Diskriminan
notes: Diskriminan an se ekspresyon andedan rasin kare fòmil kwadratik la.
Si D > 0: de solisyon reyèl distink (parabòl la travèse aks x de fwa).
Si D = 0: egzakteman yon solisyon reyèl, yon rasin repete (parabòl la touche aks x yon fwa).
Si D < 0: pa gen solisyon reyèl (parabòl la pa travèse aks x).
Ou ka klase kantite solisyon anvan rezoud — ekonomize tan nan egzamen.
